speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Young Prince of Tyre, you have at large received

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: The danger of the task you undertake.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I have, Antiochus, and with a soul

speaker: PERICLES
text: Emboldened with the glory of her praise

speaker: PERICLES
text: Think death no hazard in this enterprise.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Music!

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Bring in our daughter, clothèd like a bride

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: For embracements even of Jove himself,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: At whose conception, till Lucina reigned,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Nature this dowry gave: to glad her presence,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: The senate house of planets all did sit

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: To knit in her their best perfections.

speaker: PERICLES
text: See where she comes, appareled like the spring,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Graces her subjects, and her thoughts the king

speaker: PERICLES
text: Of every virtue gives renown to men!

speaker: PERICLES
text: Her face the book of praises, where is read

speaker: PERICLES
text: Nothing but curious pleasures, as from thence

speaker: PERICLES
text: Sorrow were ever razed, and testy wrath

speaker: PERICLES
text: Could never be her mild companion.

speaker: PERICLES
text: You gods that made me man, and sway in love,

speaker: PERICLES
text: That have inflamed desire in my breast

speaker: PERICLES
text: To taste the fruit of yon celestial tree

speaker: PERICLES
text: Or die in th’ adventure, be my helps,

speaker: PERICLES
text: As I am son and servant to your will,

speaker: PERICLES
text: To compass such a boundless happiness.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Prince Pericles—

speaker: PERICLES
text: That would be son to great Antiochus.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Before thee stands this fair Hesperides,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: With golden fruit, but dangerous to be touched;

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: For deathlike dragons here affright thee hard.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Her face, like heaven, enticeth thee to view

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Her countless glory, which desert must gain;

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: And which without desert, because thine eye

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Presumes to reach, all the whole heap must die.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Yon sometimes famous princes, like thyself,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Drawn by report, advent’rous by desire,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Tell thee with speechless tongues and semblance pale

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: That, without covering save yon field of stars,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Here they stand martyrs slain in Cupid’s wars,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: And with dead cheeks advise thee to desist

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: For going on death’s net, whom none resist.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Antiochus, I thank thee, who hath taught

speaker: PERICLES
text: My frail mortality to know itself,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And by those fearful objects to prepare

speaker: PERICLES
text: This body, like to them, to what I must.

speaker: PERICLES
text: For death remembered should be like a mirror

speaker: PERICLES
text: Who tells us life’s but breath, to trust it error.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I’ll make my will, then, and as sick men do

speaker: PERICLES
text: Who know the world, see heaven but, feeling woe,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Gripe not at earthly joys as erst they did;

speaker: PERICLES
text: So I bequeath a happy peace to you

speaker: PERICLES
text: And all good men, as every prince should do;

speaker: PERICLES
text: My riches to the earth from whence they came,

speaker: PERICLES
text:
To
                 
                the
                 
                Daughter
                .
                 
              But my unspotted fire of love to you.—

speaker: PERICLES
text: Thus ready for the way of life or death,

speaker: PERICLES
text: I wait the sharpest blow.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Scorning advice, read the conclusion, then:

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Which read and not expounded, ’tis decreed,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: As these before thee, thou thyself shalt bleed.

speaker: DAUGHTER
text: Of all ’sayed yet, mayst thou prove prosperous;

speaker: DAUGHTER
text: Of all ’sayed yet, I wish thee happiness.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Like a bold champion I assume the lists,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Nor ask advice of any other thought

speaker: PERICLES
text: But faithfulness and courage.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I am no viper, yet I feed

speaker: PERICLES
text: On mother’s flesh which did me breed.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I sought a husband, in which labor

speaker: PERICLES
text: I found that kindness in a father.

speaker: PERICLES
text: He’s father, son, and husband mild;

speaker: PERICLES
text: I mother, wife, and yet his child.

speaker: PERICLES
text: How they may be, and yet in two,

speaker: PERICLES
text: As you will live resolve it you.

speaker: PERICLES
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              Sharp physic is the last! But, O you powers

speaker: PERICLES
text: That gives heaven countless eyes to view men’s acts,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Why cloud they not their sights perpetually

speaker: PERICLES
text: If this be true which makes me pale to read it?

speaker: PERICLES
text: Fair glass of light, I loved you, and could still

speaker: PERICLES
text: Were not this glorious casket stored with ill.

speaker: PERICLES
text: But I must tell you now my thoughts revolt;

speaker: PERICLES
text: For he’s no man on whom perfections wait

speaker: PERICLES
text: That, knowing sin within, will touch the gate.

speaker: PERICLES
text: You are a fair viol, and your sense the strings

speaker: PERICLES
text: Who, fingered to make man his lawful music,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Would draw heaven down and all the gods to hearken;

speaker: PERICLES
text: But, being played upon before your time,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Hell only danceth at so harsh a chime.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Good sooth, I care not for you.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Prince Pericles, touch not, upon thy life,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: For that’s an article within our law

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: As dangerous as the rest. Your time’s expired.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Either expound now or receive your sentence.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Great king,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Few love to hear the sins they love to act.

speaker: PERICLES
text: ’Twould braid yourself too near for me to tell it.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Who has a book of all that monarchs do,

speaker: PERICLES
text: He’s more secure to keep it shut than shown.

speaker: PERICLES
text: For vice repeated is like the wand’ring wind,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Blows dust in others’ eyes to spread itself;

speaker: PERICLES
text: And yet the end of all is bought thus dear:

speaker: PERICLES
text: The breath is gone, and the sore eyes see clear

speaker: PERICLES
text: To stop the air would hurt them. The blind mole casts

speaker: PERICLES
text: Copped hills towards heaven, to tell the Earth is thronged

speaker: PERICLES
text: By man’s oppression, and the poor worm doth die for ’t.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Kings are Earth’s gods; in vice their law’s their will;

speaker: PERICLES
text: And if Jove stray, who dares say Jove doth ill?

speaker: PERICLES
text: It is enough you know; and it is fit,

speaker: PERICLES
text: What being more known grows worse, to smother it.

speaker: PERICLES
text: All love the womb that their first being bred;

speaker: PERICLES
text: Then give my tongue like leave to love my head.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Heaven, that I had thy head! He has found the meaning.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: But I will gloze with him.—Young Prince of Tyre,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Though by the tenor of our strict edict,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Your exposition misinterpreting,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: We might proceed to cancel of your days,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Yet hope, succeeding from so fair a tree

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: As your fair self, doth tune us otherwise.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Forty days longer we do respite you,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: If by which time our secret be undone,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: This mercy shows we’ll joy in such a son.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: And until then, your entertain shall be

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: As doth befit our honor and your worth.

speaker: PERICLES
text: How courtesy would seem to cover sin

speaker: PERICLES
text: When what is done is like an hypocrite,

speaker: PERICLES
text: The which is good in nothing but in sight.

speaker: PERICLES
text: If it be true that I interpret false,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Then were it certain you were not so bad

speaker: PERICLES
text: As with foul incest to abuse your soul;

speaker: PERICLES
text: Where now you’re both a father and a son

speaker: PERICLES
text: By your untimely claspings with your child,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Which pleasures fits a husband, not a father,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And she an eater of her mother’s flesh

speaker: PERICLES
text: By the defiling of her parents’ bed;

speaker: PERICLES
text: And both like serpents are, who, though they feed

speaker: PERICLES
text: On sweetest flowers, yet they poison breed.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Antioch, farewell, for wisdom sees those men

speaker: PERICLES
text: Blush not in actions blacker than the night

speaker: PERICLES
text: Will ’schew no course to keep them from the light.

speaker: PERICLES
text: One sin, I know, another doth provoke;

speaker: PERICLES
text: Murder’s as near to lust as flame to smoke.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Poison and treason are the hands of sin,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Ay, and the targets to put off the shame.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Then, lest my life be cropped to keep you clear,

speaker: PERICLES
text: By flight I’ll shun the danger which I fear.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: He hath found the meaning,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: For which we mean to have his head.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: He must not live to trumpet forth my infamy,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Nor tell the world Antiochus doth sin

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: In such a loathèd manner.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: And therefore instantly this prince must die,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: For by his fall my honor must keep high.—

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Who attends us there?

speaker: THALIARD
text: Doth your Highness call?

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Thaliard, you are of our chamber, Thaliard,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: And our mind partakes her private actions

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: To your secrecy; and for your faithfulness

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: We will advance you, Thaliard. Behold,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Here’s poison, and here’s gold.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: We hate the Prince

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Of Tyre, and thou must kill him. It fits thee not

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: To ask the reason why: because we bid it.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Say, is it done?

speaker: THALIARD
text: My lord, ’tis done.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Enough.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Let your breath cool yourself, telling your haste.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: My lord, Prince Pericles is fled.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: As thou wilt live, fly after,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: and like an arrow shot from a well-experienced

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: archer hits the mark his eye doth level at, so thou

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: never return unless thou say Prince Pericles is

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: dead.

speaker: THALIARD
text: My lord, if I can get him within my pistol’s

speaker: THALIARD
text: length, I’ll make him sure enough. So, farewell to

speaker: THALIARD
text: your Highness.

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: Thaliard, adieu. Till Pericles be dead,

speaker: ANTIOCHUS
text: My heart can lend no succor to my head.
